prevalence.source: Indian aging cohort syntheses / ARDSI context
prevalence.trend: increasing with rapid aging and vascular risk factor growth

vulnerablePopulations: rural populations with limited specialist access
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Cognitive concern with objective impairment and preserved independence; screening tools (HMSE/MMSE/MoCA) and evaluation for reversible causes

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: low; cognitive screening not routine in most primary care settings
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low to moderate; risk-factor control limited by access and continuity gaps

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Diagnosis concentrated in urban tertiary centers; many cases not formally diagnosed
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Risk-factor control, cognitive stimulation, physical activity, treat depression/hearing loss when possible; medication review
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Underdiagnosis, limited neuropsychological testing, and low access to memory clinics outside metros
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Family caregiving networks and expanding NCD programs can support vascular risk factor management
